title: Extraction Method A
author: Sarah Siu Tze Mak
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.iabcaan

steps.instruction: Digestion in a proteinase K containing buffer following Gilbert et al. 2007. Gilbert MTP, Tomsho LP, Rendulic S, Packard M, Drautz DI, Sher A, et al. Whole-genome shotgun sequencing of mitochondria from ancient hair shafts. Science. American Association for the Advancement of Science; 2007;317:1927–30.
Pre-digest all samples at 56 °C for 1 hour. 01:00:00 Perform incubation with a rotation of the samples in an oven.
Replace the buffer with fresh buffer.
Perform a second 12-hour digest with the fresh buffer. 12:00:00 Perform incubation with a rotation of the samples in an oven.
Purify digests using organic solvents (phenol:chloroform) and Qiagen MinElute columns (Qiagen, Hilden, DE) as described in the following steps:

Mix digests with 8x modified PB buffer (Allentoft et al. 2015). Allentoft ME, Sikora M, Sjögren K-G, Rasmussen S, Rasmussen M, Stenderup J, et al. Population genomics of Bronze Age Eurasia. Nature. Nature Publishing Group; Dabney J, Knapp M, Glocke I et al. (2013) Complete mitochondrial genome sequence of a Middle Pleistocene cave bear reconstructed from ultrashort DNA fragments. Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America, 110, 15758–15763.2015;522:167–72.
Bind the digests to the MinElute column using reservoirs as previously described (Dabney et al. 2013).
Incubate for 5 minutes at 37 °C prior to release. (1/2) 00:05:00
Incubate for 5 minutes at 37 °C prior to release. (2/2) 00:05:00

Prior to library construction, analyze small aliquots of each extract on an Agilent 2200 TapeStation HS chip (Agilent Technologies, Palo Alto, California, USA) for fragment size estimation and molar concentration.
